libraryPreparationPreflight.agent_guidance: No environment variables or system properties are required. Update tests for the 2.38.0 API: org.apache.activemq.artemis.utils.Base64 exposes encodeObject(Serializable), encodeBytes(byte[][, boolean]), and decode(String[, boolean]); it no longer exposes DONT_BREAK_LINES or decodeToObject(String). If covering FactoryFinder, ClassloadingUtil, PasswordMaskingUtil service loading, or XmlProvider schema resolution, create ordinary local test resources under src/test/resources as needed; do not assume a running Artemis broker.

input_evidence.name: issue
input_evidence.summary: #8665 [Automation] javac compile fails for org.apache.activemq:artemis-commons:2.38.0; label=fails-javac-compile; body_chars=8015

risks: Existing 2.34.0 tests may need source edits for API changes in 2.38.0 before Gradle compile can validate coverage.
XmlProvider has an optional artemis.disableXxe system property, but default behavior is testable without setting it; only set/reset it inside a specific test if explicitly covering the XXE toggle.
Native Image execution may still reveal reachability metadata gaps after the javac issue is fixed.
libraryPreparationPreflight.summary: org.apache.activemq:artemis-commons is an in-process ActiveMQ Artemis utility/API artifact; its compile-time dependencies are declared transitively and meaningful tests do not require a broker, Docker service, extra Maven dependency, or global environment setup. The reported javac failure is caused by removed Base64 APIs in 2.38.0, not missing setup.
